seasonal_closures: Most rental homes available April-November only; some restaurants close November-March
highlights: Wright Brothers National Memorial; Jockey's Ridge State Park (largest sand dune on East Coast); Cape Hatteras National Seashore; Wild horse tours in Corolla; Fresh seafood at local fish houses; Kiteboarding and surfing; Roanoke Island and The Lost Colony outdoor drama
source_names: TripAdvisor - Outer Banks; Google Reviews; Expedia - OBX Vacation Rentals; OuterBanks.org
notes: Vacation rental (full house) is more common than hotel here. The $85/person/night estimate assumes a 4-person share of a $340/night rental house. Hotel rates in Kill Devil Hills run $150-250/night for two.
research_notes: Cost estimate is from June 2025 peak-season search. Shoulder season (April-May, September-October) can be 30-40% cheaper. Should re-check pricing for summer 2026 closer to travel dates. TripAdvisor and Expedia ratings agree well; Google is lower, possibly reflecting off-season visitor reviews.

highlights: Canaan Valley Ski Resort (47 trails); Blackwater Falls State Park; Dolly Sods Wilderness hiking; Mountain biking at Timberline; Scenic drives along Highland Scenic Highway; Stargazing (Bortle 2-3 dark sky area)
source_names: TripAdvisor - Canaan Valley Resort; WV Tourism Blog; Google Reviews; Expedia
notes: State park resort, so prices are reasonable. Lodge rooms and cabins available. The area is remote -- limited cell service and few restaurants outside the resort.
research_notes: DISCREPANCY: Travel time listed as 3.0 hours based on WV Tourism blog. Checked Google Maps on 2026-03-20: Richmond VA to Davis WV is actually 4 hours 35 minutes via I-64W and US-33W. The blog likely measured from the DC area, not Richmond. This needs to be corrected -- travel_time_hours should be ~4.5. Also, pricing was checked September 2025; the resort's 2026 rate card has not been verified. The resort changed management in late 2025 which may affect rates.

highlights: Historic district walking tours; Rainbow Row (iconic painted houses); Fort Sumter National Monument; Charleston City Market; World-class dining (Husk, FIG, Slightly North of Broad); Folly Beach (20 min drive); Magnolia Plantation and Gardens; Waterfront Park and Pineapple Fountain
source_names: TripAdvisor - Charleston; Google Reviews - Charleston Hotels; Yelp - Charleston; Booking.com - Charleston Hotels; National Park Service - Fort Sumter; Travel + Leisure - Best Cities 2025
notes: Charleston is consistently ranked among the top US travel destinations. Hotel prices vary widely -- the $140/person estimate is based on a mid-range hotel (Holiday Inn, Hampton Inn) split between two people. Boutique hotels in the historic district run $300-500/night. Street parking is difficult; budget $20-30/day for garage parking.
research_notes: This is the cleanest entry in the dataset. All three review sources agree closely (4.5-4.7 range). Cost was verified on Booking.com for a May 2026 weekend stay. Travel time confirmed via Google Maps. Multiple independent editorial sources (Travel + Leisure, Conde Nast Traveler) corroborate the highlights. No known discrepancies.

seasonal_closures: Skyline Drive sections close during ice/snow (typically December-March); some facilities close November-March
highlights: Skyline Drive (105-mile scenic road); Old Rag Mountain hike (rock scramble summit); Dark Hollow Falls (easiest waterfall hike); Big Meadows Lodge and campground; Luray Caverns (nearby, privately operated); Fall foliage (peak mid-October); Appalachian Trail access (101 miles through the park)
source_names: TripAdvisor - Shenandoah National Park; Google Reviews; National Park Service - Shenandoah; Recreation.gov - Big Meadows Campground; Skyland Resort Official
notes: Cost estimate based on Big Meadows Lodge at $130/night for two people. Camping is much cheaper ($30/night). Skyland Resort rooms start at $180/night. Park entrance fee is $30/vehicle (or free with America the Beautiful pass). Old Rag requires a day-use ticket ($1/person) booked in advance.
research_notes: DISCREPANCY: TripAdvisor ratings for the park are 4.5 but Google Reviews show 4.7. The gap likely reflects different review populations -- TripAdvisor skews toward tourists who may be comparing to western national parks, while Google reviews include more regional visitors. NPS visitor satisfaction survey (2024) shows 4.4/5.0 overall satisfaction, which pulls the average down. All three are legitimate but they measure different things. Noted but not a data quality issue per se -- just worth understanding when presenting the average.

seasonal_closures: Ski slopes open December-March only; outdoor pool open Memorial Day-Labor Day; some restaurants seasonal
highlights: Skiing and snowboarding (26 trails); Golf (45 holes, Devil's Knob and Stoney Creek); Spa and indoor pool; Mountain biking; Blue Ridge Parkway access; Shamokin Springs nature preserve; Brewing and winery tours in Nelson County
source_names: TripAdvisor - Wintergreen Resort; Google Reviews; Wintergreen Resort Official
notes: Wintergreen is a condo-style resort -- you rent a privately-owned condo through the resort's management company. Quality varies significantly by unit. The $110/person/night is based on a 2-bedroom condo split two ways. Studio units are cheaper but very small.
research_notes: MULTIPLE ISSUES: (1) RATING DISCREPANCY: TripAdvisor shows 3.5 while Google shows 4.0. The 0.5 gap is significant. TripAdvisor reviews frequently cite inconsistent condo quality and high resort fees. Google reviews skew toward day-trip skiers who rate the mountain, not the lodging. These are measuring different experiences. (2) OUTDATED PRICING: The $110/night was checked April 2025. The resort website was last accessed over a year ago. Wintergreen raised condo management fees in late 2025 which likely increased nightly rates by 10-15%. Current pricing should be verified on the resort site or Booking.com before recommending. (3) SEASONAL CLOSURES: The resort's own website mentions that the spa closed for renovation in January 2026 with no reopening date announced. A travel blog post from February 2026 still lists the spa as a highlight. The blog post is now inaccurate. This entry needs a full refresh before it can be upgraded to CONFIRMED.
